Block.Para: An Act to amend the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act (credit and debit cards)

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act to provide for the Superintendent to monitor and make recommendations relating to the use of credit and debit cards in Canada.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (credit and debit cards)

Block.Para: An Act to amend the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act (credit and debit cards)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. Section 3.1 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act is renumbered as subsection 3.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

ExplNote.Para: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act

Block.MarginalNote: Oversight body relating to credit and debit cards

Para.change: (2) An additional purpose of this Act is to provide for an oversight body to monitor and make recommendations relating to the use of credit and debit cards in Canada, as provided for under section 7.2.

Block.Para: 2. The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7.1:

Para.change: (a) monitor and publish information relating to the use of credit and debit cards in Canada, including the operation of the payment system relating to such cards, the fees and charges relating to such cards and the privacy of the users of such cards; and
(b) make such recommendations as the Superintendent considers advisable, including recommendations for changes to the law.
(2) In carrying out the duties set out in subsection (1), the Superintendent shall
(a) consult with the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada; and
(b) consult with provincial authorities with respect to provincial areas of responsibility.

Block.MarginalNote: No restriction relating to consumer provisions

Para.change: (3) In carrying out the duties set out in subsection (1), the Superintendent is not restricted by subsection 6(1) from examining and reporting on matters connected with the administration of consumer provisions as defined in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act.

Block.MarginalNote: Report on credit and debit cards

Para.change: 7.3 (1) Within two months after the end of each fiscal year, the Superintendent shall submit a report to the Minister respecting the duties set out in subsection 7.2(1).

(2) The report shall include the recommendations under paragraph 7.2(1)(b) and information about what the government has done with respect to the recommendations under that paragraph from previous years.
(3) The Minister shall respond to the Superintendent with respect to the recommendations under paragraph 7.2(1)(b).
(4) The report and the response of the Minister under subsection (3) shall be included in the report referred to in section 40.

Block.Para:
Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
